Ability.display: If false, this ability does not show in unit stats.

Accelerator.launchBlock: Core block that is launched. Should match the starting core of the planet being launched to.
Accelerator.launchCandidates: Override for planets that this block can launch to. If null, the planet's launch candidates are used.

ArmorPlateAbility.color: Color of the shine. If null, uses team color.
ArmorPlateAbility.drawPlate: Whether to draw the plate region.
ArmorPlateAbility.drawShine: Whether to draw the shine over the plate region.

CoreBlock.allowSpawn: If false, players can't respawn at this core.
CoreBlock.requiresCoreZone: If true, this core type requires a core zone to upgrade.

ContinuousTurret.aimChangeSpeed: Speed at which the turret can change its bullet "aim" distance. This is only used for point laser bullets.

BulletType.lifeScaleRandMin: Min/max multipliers for lifetime applied to this bullet when spawned.
BulletType.lifeScaleRandMax: Min/max multipliers for lifetime applied to this bullet when spawned.
BulletType.velocityScaleRandMin: Min/max multipliers for velocity applied to this bullet when spawned.
BulletType.velocityScaleRandMax: Min/max multipliers for velocity applied to this bullet when spawned.
BulletType.angleOffset: Angle offset applied to bullet when spawned each time.
BulletType.randomAngleOffset: Angle offset applied to bullet when spawned each time.
BulletType.pierceDamageFactor: Multiplier of damage decreased per health pierced.
BulletType.maxDamageFraction: If positive, limits non-splash damage dealt to a fraction of the target's maximum health.
BulletType.removeAfterPierce: If false, this bullet isn't removed after pierceCap is exceeded. Expert usage only.
BulletType.laserAbsorb: For piercing lasers, setting this to true makes it get absorbed by plastanium walls.
BulletType.optimalLifeFract: Life fraction at which this bullet has the best range/damage/etc. Used for lasers and continuous turrets.
BulletType.shootPattern: Pattern used to shoot this bullet. If null, uses turret's default pattern.
BulletType.chargeEffect: Effect created when charging starts; only usable in single-shot weapons with a firstShotDelay / shotDelay.
BulletType.smokeEffect: Extra smoke effect created when shooting.
BulletType.shootSound: Overrides the shoot sound in turrets if set. Does nothing in units, as they can't have multiple ammo types.
BulletType.hitSound: Sound made when hitting something or getting removed.
BulletType.despawnSound: Sound made when hitting something or getting removed.
BulletType.hitSoundPitch: Pitch of the sound made when hitting something
BulletType.hitSoundPitchRange: Pitch of the sound made when hitting something
BulletType.hitSoundVolume: Volume of the sound made when hitting something
BulletType.ammoMultiplier: How many bullets get created per ammo item/liquid.
BulletType.reloadMultiplier: Multiplied by turret reload speed to get final shoot speed.
BulletType.buildingDamageMultiplier: Multiplier of how much base damage is done to tiles.
BulletType.shieldDamageMultiplier: Multiplier of how much base damage is done to force shields.
BulletType.killShooter: Whether to kill the shooter when this is shot. For suicide bombers.
BulletType.instantDisappear: Whether to instantly make the bullet disappear.
BulletType.splashDamage: Damage dealt in splash. 0 to disable.
BulletType.scaledSplashDamage: If true, splash damage is "correctly" affected by unit hitbox size. Used for projectiles that do not collide / have splash as their main source of damage.
BulletType.impact: Should knockback follow the bullet's direction
BulletType.statusDuration: Intensity of applied status effect in terms of duration.
BulletType.targetBlocks: Turret only. If false, blocks will not be targeted.
BulletType.targetMissiles: Turret only. If false, missiles will not be targeted.
BulletType.collidesTiles: Whether this bullet type collides with tiles.
BulletType.collidesTeam: Whether this bullet type collides with tiles that are of the same team.
BulletType.collidesAir: Whether this bullet type collides with air/ground units.
BulletType.collidesGround: Whether this bullet type collides with air/ground units.
BulletType.collides: Whether this bullet types collides with anything at all.
BulletType.collideFloor: If true, this projectile collides with non-surface floors.
BulletType.collideTerrain: If true, this projectile collides with static walls
BulletType.keepVelocity: Whether velocity is inherited from the shooter.
BulletType.scaleLife: Whether to scale lifetime (not actually velocity!) to disappear at the target position. Used for artillery.
BulletType.hittable: Whether this bullet can be hit by point defense.
BulletType.reflectable: Whether this bullet can be reflected.
BulletType.absorbable: Whether this projectile can be absorbed by shields.
BulletType.ignoreSpawnAngle: If true, the angle param in create is ignored.
BulletType.createChance: Chance for this bullet to be created.
BulletType.rangeOverride: When > 0, overrides range even if smaller than base range.
BulletType.rangeChange: When used in a turret with multiple ammo types, this can be set to a non-zero value to influence range.
BulletType.extraRangeMargin: When used in turrets with limitRange() applied, this adds extra range to the bullets that extends past targeting range. Only particularly relevant in vanilla.
BulletType.minRangeChange: When used in a turret with multiple ammo types, this can be set to a non-zero value to influence minRange
BulletType.healAmount: flat amount of block health healed
BulletType.healSound: sound played when a block is healed
BulletType.lifesteal: Fraction of bullet damage that heals that shooter.
BulletType.makeFire: Whether to make fire on impact
BulletType.hitUnder: Whether this bullet will always hit blocks under it.
BulletType.despawnHit: Whether to create hit effects on despawn. Forced to true if this bullet has any special effects like splash damage. Disable setDefaults to avoid override
BulletType.fragOnHit: If true, this bullet will create bullets when it hits anything
BulletType.fragOnDespawn: If true, this bullet will create bullets when it despawns
BulletType.fragOnAbsorb: If false, this bullet will not create frags when absorbed by a shield.
BulletType.pierceArmor: If true, unit armor is ignored in damage calculations.
BulletType.armorMultiplier: Multiplies the unit armor used in damage calculations. Used for armor weakness, armor piercing, and anti-armor.
BulletType.sticky: If true, the bullet will "stick" to enemies and get deactivated on collision.
BulletType.stickyExtraLifetime: Extra time added to bullet when it sticks to something.
BulletType.setDefaults: Whether status and despawnHit should automatically be set.
BulletType.hitShake: Amount of shaking produced when this bullet hits something or despawns.
BulletType.despawnShake: Amount of shaking produced when this bullet hits something or despawns.
BulletType.fragBullet: Bullet type that is created when this bullet expires.
BulletType.delayFrags: If true, frag bullets are delayed to the next frame. Fixes obscure bugs with piercing bullet types spawning frags immediately and screwing up the Damage temporary variables.
BulletType.fragRandomSpread: Degree spread range of fragmentation bullets.
BulletType.fragSpread: Uniform spread between each frag bullet in degrees.
BulletType.fragVelocityMin: Random range of frag velocity as a multiplier.
BulletType.fragVelocityMax: Random range of frag velocity as a multiplier.
BulletType.fragLifeMin: Random range of frag lifetime as a multiplier.
BulletType.fragLifeMax: Random range of frag lifetime as a multiplier.
BulletType.fragOffsetMin: Random offset of frag bullets from the parent bullet.
BulletType.fragOffsetMax: Random offset of frag bullets from the parent bullet.
BulletType.pierceFragCap: How many times this bullet can release frag bullets, if pierce = true.
BulletType.intervalBullet: Bullet that is created at a fixed interval.
BulletType.bulletInterval: Interval, in ticks, between which bullet spawn.
BulletType.intervalBullets: Number of bullet spawned per interval.
BulletType.intervalRandomSpread: Random angle added to interval bullets.
BulletType.intervalSpread: Angle spread between individual interval bullets.
BulletType.intervalDelay: Use a negative value to disable interval bullet delay.
BulletType.underwater: If true, this bullet is rendered underwater. Highly experimental!
BulletType.healColor: Color used for block heal effects.
BulletType.healEffect: Effect emitted upon blocks that are healed.
BulletType.spawnBullets: Bullets spawned when this bullet is created. Rarely necessary, used for visuals.
BulletType.showStats: Whether to display the stats of the spawned bullet.
BulletType.spawnBulletRandomSpread: Random angle spread of spawn bullets.
BulletType.spawnUnit: Unit spawned instead of this bullet. Useful for missiles.
BulletType.despawnUnit: Unit spawned when this bullet hits something or despawns due to it hitting the end of its lifetime.
BulletType.despawnUnitChance: The chance for despawn units to spawn.
BulletType.despawnUnitCount: Amount of units spawned when this bullet despawns.
BulletType.despawnUnitRadius: Random offset distance from the original bullet despawn/hit coordinate.
BulletType.faceOutwards: If true, units spawned when this bullet despawns face away from the bullet instead of the same direction as the bullet.
BulletType.parts: Extra visual parts for this bullet.
BulletType.trailChance: Chance of trail effect spawning on bullet per tick.
BulletType.trailInterval: Uniform interval in which trail effect is spawned.
BulletType.trailMinVelocity: Min velocity required for trail effect to spawn.
BulletType.trailParam: Rotation/size parameter that is passed to trail. Usually, this controls size.
BulletType.trailRotation: Whether the parameter passed to the trail is the bullet rotation, instead of a flat value.
BulletType.trailInterp: Interpolation for trail width as function of bullet lifetime
BulletType.trailLength: Length of trail quads. Any value <= 0 disables the trail.
BulletType.trailSinMag: If trailSinMag > 0, these values are applied as a sine curve to trail width.
BulletType.trailSinScl: If trailSinMag > 0, these values are applied as a sine curve to trail width.
BulletType.circleShooter: If true, the bullet will attempt to circle around its shooting entity.
BulletType.circleShooterRadius: Radius that the bullet attempts to circle at.
BulletType.circleShooterRadiusSmooth: Smooth extra radius value for circling.
BulletType.circleShooterRotateSpeed: Multiplier of speed that is used to adjust velocity when circling.
BulletType.splashDamageRadius: Use a negative value to disable splash damage.
BulletType.splashDamagePierce: If true, splash damage pierces through tiles.
BulletType.incendAmount: Amount of fires attempted around bullet.
BulletType.homingPower: Power of bullet ability. Usually a number between 0 and 1; try 0.1 as a starting point.
BulletType.homingRange: Range of homing effect around bullet.
BulletType.homingDelay: Use a negative value to disable homing delay.
BulletType.followAimSpeed: Speed at which bullet rotates to follow cursor. <= 0 to disable.
BulletType.suppressionRange: Range of healing block suppression effect.
BulletType.suppressionDuration: Duration of healing block suppression effect.
BulletType.suppressionEffectChance: Chance of suppression effect occurring on block, scaled down by number of blocks.
BulletType.suppressColor: Color used for the regenSuppressSeek effect.
BulletType.lightningColor: Color of lightning created by bullet.
BulletType.lightningLengthRand: Extra random length added onto base length of lightning.
BulletType.lightningDamage: Use a negative value to use default bullet damage.
BulletType.lightningCone: Spread of lightning, relative to bullet rotation.
BulletType.lightningAngle: Offset of lightning relative to bullet rotation.
BulletType.lightningType: The bullet created at lightning points.
BulletType.weaveScale: Scale of bullet weave pattern. Higher -> less vibration.
BulletType.weaveMag: Intensity of bullet weaving. Note that this may make bullets inaccurate.
BulletType.weaveRandom: If true, the bullet weave will randomly switch directions on spawn.
BulletType.rotateSpeed: Rotation speed of the bullet velocity as it travels.
BulletType.puddleRange: Range of puddles around bullet position.
BulletType.puddleAmount: Liquid count of each puddle created.
BulletType.puddleLiquid: Liquid that puddles created are made of.
BulletType.displayAmmoMultiplier: Whether to display the ammo multiplayer for this bullet type in its stats.
BulletType.statLiquidConsumed: If >0, this is displayed divided by the ammo multiplier.
BulletType.lightRadius: Radius of light emitted by this bullet; <0 to use defaults.
BulletType.lightColor: Color of light emitted by this bullet.

ContinuousFlameBulletType.lengthWidthPans: Lengths, widths, ellipse panning, and offsets, all as fractions of the base width and length. Stored as an 'interleaved' array of values: LWPO1 LWPO2 LWPO3...

ContinuousBulletType.timescaleDamage: If a building fired this, whether to multiply damage by its timescale.

ConsumeGenerator.itemDuration: The time in number of ticks during which a single item will produce power.
ConsumeGenerator.explodeOnFull: If true, this block explodes when outputLiquid exceeds capacity.
ConsumeGenerator.itemDurationMultipliers: Multiplies the itemDuration for a given item.

Conduit.padCorners: If true, the liquid region is padded at corners, so it doesn't stick out.

Block.hasItems: If true, buildings have an ItemModule.
Block.hasLiquids: If true, buildings have a LiquidModule.
Block.hasPower: If true, buildings have a PowerModule.
Block.outputsLiquid: Flag for determining whether this block outputs liquid somewhere; used for connections.
Block.consumesPower: Used by certain power blocks (nodes) to flag as non-consuming of power. True by default, even if this block has no power.
Block.outputsPower: If true, this block is a generator that can produce power.
Block.connectedPower: If false, power nodes cannot connect to this block.
Block.conductivePower: If true, this block can conduct power like a cable.
Block.outputsPayload: If true, this block can output payloads; affects blending.
Block.acceptsUnitPayloads: If true, this block can input payloads; affects unit payload enter behavior.
Block.acceptsPayload: If true, payloads will attempt to move into this block.
Block.acceptsItems: Visual flag use for blending of certain transportation blocks.
Block.alwaysAllowDeposit: If true, this block won't be affected by the onlyDepositCore rule.
Block.depositCooldown: Cooldown, in seconds, applied to player item depositing when any item is deposited to this block. Overrides the itemDepositCooldown if non-negative.
Block.separateItemCapacity: If true, all item capacities of this block are separate instead of pooled as one number.
Block.itemCapacity: maximum items this block can carry (usually, this is per-type of item)
Block.liquidCapacity: maximum total liquids this block can carry if hasLiquids = true. Default value is 10, scales with max liquid consumption in ConsumeLiquid
Block.liquidPressure: higher numbers increase liquid output speed; TODO remove and replace with better liquids system
Block.outputFacing: If true, this block outputs to its facing direction, when applicable. Used for blending calculations.
Block.noSideBlend: if true, this block does not accept input from the sides (used for armored conveyors)
Block.inEditor: whether this block is visible in the editor
Block.editorConfigurable: if true, {@link #buildEditorConfig(Table)} will be called for configuring this block in the editor.
Block.lastConfig: the last configuration value applied to this block.
Block.saveConfig: whether to save the last config and apply it to newly placed blocks
Block.copyConfig: whether to allow copying the config through middle click
Block.clearOnDoubleTap: if true, double-tapping this configurable block clears configuration.
Block.update: whether this block has a tile entity that updates
Block.destructible: whether this block has health and can be destroyed. note that setting this to false does nothing if update = true!
Block.unloadable: whether unloaders work on this block
Block.isDuct: if true, this block acts a duct and will connect to armored ducts from the side.
Block.allowResupply: whether units can resupply by taking items from this block
Block.solidifes: whether this block CAN be solid.
Block.teamPassable: if true, this counts as a non-solid block to this team.
Block.underBullets: if true, this block cannot be hit by bullets unless explicitly targeted.
Block.rotateDraw: if rotate is true and this is false, the region won't rotate when drawing
Block.rotateDrawEditor: if rotate is true and this is false, the region won't rotate when drawing in the editor
Block.visualRotationOffset: visual rotation offset used in broken plan rendering
Block.lockRotation: if rotate = false and this is true, rotation will be locked at 0 when placing (default); advanced use only
Block.ignoreLineRotation: if true, this block won't face the line drag direction
Block.invertFlip: if true, schematic flips with this block are inverted.
Block.variants: number of different variant regions to use
Block.drawArrow: whether to draw a rotation arrow - this does not apply to lines of blocks
Block.drawTeamOverlay: whether to draw the team corner by default
Block.saveData: for static blocks only: if true, tile data() is saved in world data.
Block.breakable: whether you can break this with rightclick
Block.unitMoveBreakable: if true, this block will be broken by certain units stepping/moving over it
Block.rebuildable: whether to add this block to brokenblocks
Block.privileged: if true, this logic-related block can only be used with privileged processors (or is one itself)
Block.requiresWater: whether this block can only be placed on water
Block.placeableLiquid: whether this block can be placed on any liquids, anywhere
Block.placeablePlayer: whether this block can be placed directly by the player via PlacementFragment
Block.placeableOn: whether this floor can be placed on.
Block.insulated: whether this block has insulating properties.
Block.squareSprite: whether the sprite is a full square.
Block.absorbLasers: whether this block absorbs laser attacks.
Block.enableDrawStatus: if false, the status is never drawn
Block.autoResetEnabled: whether to automatically reset enabled status after a logic block has not interacted for a while.
Block.noUpdateDisabled: if true, the block stops updating when disabled
Block.updateInUnits: if true, this block updates when it's a payload in a unit.
Block.alwaysUpdateInUnits: if true, this block updates in payloads in units regardless of the experimental game rule
Block.canPickup: if true, this block can be picked up in payloads
Block.deconstructDropAllLiquid: if false, only incinerable liquids are dropped when deconstructing; otherwise, all liquids are dropped.
Block.useColor: Whether to use this block's color in the minimap. Only used for overlays.
Block.itemDrop: item that drops from this block, used for drills
Block.playerUnmineable: if true, this block cannot be mined by players. useful for annoying things like sand.
Block.attributes: Array of affinities to certain things.
Block.scaledHealth: Health per square tile that this block occupies; essentially, this is multiplied by size * size. Overridden if health is > 0. If <0, the default is 40.
Block.armor: damage absorption, similar to unit armor
Block.explosivenessScale: scaling of explosiveness based on items/liquids
Block.flammabilityScale: scaling of explosion flammability based on items/liquids
Block.baseShake: base value for screen shake upon destruction
Block.destroyBullet: bullet that this block spawns when destroyed
Block.destroyBulletSameTeam: if true, destroyBullet is spawned on the block's team instead of Derelict team
Block.drawCracks: whether cracks are drawn when this block is damaged
Block.createRubble: whether rubble is created when this block is destroyed
Block.floating: whether this block can be placed on edges of liquids.
Block.clipSize: Clipping size of this block. Should be as large as the block will draw.
Block.placeOverlapRange: When placeRangeCheck is enabled, this is the range checked for enemy blocks.
Block.crushDamageMultiplier: Multiplier of damage dealt to this block by tanks. Does not apply to crawlers.
Block.crushFragile: If true, this block is instantly destroyed by tanks with crushFragile set to true.
Block.cacheLayer: Cache layer. Only used for 'cached' rendering.
Block.fillsTile: Special flag; if false, floor will be drawn under this block even if it is cached.
Block.forceDark: If true, this block can be covered by darkness / fog even if synthetic.
Block.alwaysReplace: whether this block can be replaced in all cases
Block.replaceable: if false, this block can never be replaced.
Block.group: The block group. Unless {@link #canReplace} is overridden, blocks in the same group can replace each other.
Block.flags: List of block flags. Used for AI indexing.
Block.priority: Targeting priority of this block, as seen by enemies.
Block.unitCapModifier: How much this block affects the unit cap by. The block flags must contain unitModifier in order for this to work.
Block.configurable: Whether the block can be tapped and selected to configure.
Block.configureSound: Sound played when this block is configured.
Block.ignoreResizeConfig: If true, this block does not have pointConfig with a transform called on map resize.
Block.commandable: If true, this building can be selected like a unit when commanding.
Block.allowConfigInventory: If true, the building inventory can be shown with the config.
Block.selectionRows: Defines how large selection menus, such as that of sorters, should be.
Block.selectionColumns: Defines how large selection menus, such as that of sorters, should be.
Block.logicConfigurable: If true, this block can be configured by logic.
Block.delayLandingConfig: If true, configuration is delayed when playing the landing block buildup animation. This may be removed in the future!
Block.consumesTap: Whether this block consumes touchDown events when tapped.
Block.drawLiquidLight: Whether to draw the glow of the liquid for this block, if it has one.
Block.envRequired: Environmental flags that are all required for this block to function. 0 = any environment
Block.envEnabled: The environment flags that this block can function in. If the env matches any of these, it will be enabled.
Block.envDisabled: The environment flags that this block cannot function in. If the env matches any of these, it will be disabled.
Block.sync: Whether to periodically sync this block across the network.
Block.conveyorPlacement: Whether this block uses conveyor-type placement mode.
Block.allowDiagonal: If false, diagonal placement (ctrl) for this block is not allowed.
Block.swapDiagonalPlacement: Whether to swap the diagonal placement modes.
Block.allowRectanglePlacement: Whether to allow rectangular placement, as opposed to a line.
Block.mapColor: The color of this block when displayed on the minimap or map preview. Do not set manually! This is overridden when loading for most blocks.
Block.hasColor: Whether this block has a minimap color.
Block.attacks: If true, this block attacks and is considered a turret in the indexer. Building must implement Ranged.
Block.suppressable: If true, this block is mending-related and can be suppressed with special units/missiles.
Block.canOverdrive: Whether the overdrive core has any effect on this block.
Block.outlineIcon: Whether any icon region has an outline added.
Block.outlinedIcon: Which of the icon regions gets the outline added. Uses last icon if <= 0.
Block.hasShadow: Whether this block has a shadow under it.
Block.customShadow: If true, a custom shadow (name-shadow) is drawn under this block.
Block.placePitchChange: Should the sound made when this block is built change in pitch.
Block.breakPitchChange: Should the sound made when this block is deconstructed change in pitch.
Block.placeSound: Sound made when this block is built.
Block.breakSound: Sound made when this block is deconstructed.
Block.destroySound: Sounds made when this block is destroyed.
Block.emitLight: If true, drawLight() will be called for this block.
Block.obstructsLight: If true, this block obstructs light emitted by other blocks.
Block.lightRadius: Radius of the light emitted by this block.
Block.fogRadius: How much fog this block uncovers, in tiles. Cannot be dynamic. <= 0 to disable.
Block.ambientSound: The sound that this block makes while idle. Uses one sound loop for all blocks.
Block.buildTime: Time to build this block in ticks. If this value is <0, it is calculated dynamically.
Block.buildVisibility: Whether this block is visible and can currently be built.
Block.buildCostMultiplier: Multiplier for speed of building this block.
Block.deconstructThreshold: Build completion at which deconstruction finishes.
Block.instantDeconstruct: If true, this block deconstructs immediately. Instant deconstruction implies no resource refund.
Block.instantBuild: If true, this block constructs immediately. This implies no resource requirement, and ignores configs - do not use, this is for performance only!
Block.ignoreBuildDarkness: If true, this block can be placed even in "dark" areas. Only used for editor static walls.
Block.placeEffect: Effect for placing the block. Passes size as rotation.
Block.breakEffect: Effect for breaking the block. Passes size as rotation.
Block.researchCostMultiplier: Multiplier for cost of research in tech tree.
Block.researchCost: Override for research cost. Uses multipliers above and building requirements if not set.
Block.forceTeam: If set, all blocks will be forced to be this team.
Block.instantTransfer: Whether this block has instant transfer.
Block.quickRotate: Whether you can rotate this block after it is placed.
Block.allowDerelictRepair: If true, this derelict block can be repair by clicking it.
Block.buildType: Building that is created for this block. Initialized in init() via reflection. Set manually if modded.
Block.consumers: Array of consumers used by this block. Only populated after init().
Block.optionalConsumers: Array of consumers used by this block. Only populated after init().
Block.nonOptionalConsumers: Array of consumers used by this block. Only populated after init().
Block.updateConsumers: Array of consumers used by this block. Only populated after init().
Block.hasConsumers: Set to true if this block has any consumers in its array.
Block.consPower: The single power consumer, if applicable.
Block.regionRotated1: Regions indexes from icons() that are rotated. If either of these is not -1, other regions won't be rotated in ConstructBlocks.
Block.regionRotated2: Regions indexes from icons() that are rotated. If either of these is not -1, other regions won't be rotated in ConstructBlocks.
Block.dumpTime: How often to try dumping items in ticks, e.g. 5 = 12 times/sec

BeamDrill.optionalBoostIntensity: How many times faster the drill will progress when boosted by an optional consumer.
BeamDrill.drillMultipliers: Multipliers of drill speed for each item. Defaults to 1.
BeamDrill.blockedItem: Special exemption item that this drill can't mine.
BeamDrill.blockedItems: Special exemption items that this drill can't mine.

DrawFrames.sine: If true, frames wil alternate back and forth in a sine wave.

BaseTurret.activationTime: How much time to start shooting after placement.
BaseTurret.coolEffect: Effect displayed when coolant is used.
BaseTurret.coolantMultiplier: How much reload is lowered by for each unit of liquid of heat capacity.
BaseTurret.coolant: If not null, this consumer will be used for coolant.

Drill.tier: Maximum tier of blocks this drill can mine.
Drill.drillTime: Base time to drill one ore, in frames.
Drill.liquidBoostIntensity: How many times faster the drill will progress when boosted by liquid.
Drill.warmupSpeed: Speed at which the drill speeds up.
Drill.blockedItem: Special exemption item that this drill can't mine.
Drill.blockedItems: Special exemption items that this drill can't mine.
Drill.drawMineItem: Whether to draw the item this drill is mining.
Drill.drillEffect: Effect played when an item is produced. This is colored.
Drill.drillEffectRnd: Drill effect randomness. Block size by default.
Drill.drillEffectChance: Chance of displaying the effect. Useful for extremely fast drills.
Drill.rotateSpeed: Speed the drill bit rotates at.
Drill.updateEffectChance: Chance the update effect will appear.
Drill.drillMultipliers: Multipliers of drill speed for each item. Defaults to 1.

DrawTurret.basePrefix: Prefix to use when loading base region.
DrawTurret.liquidDraw: Overrides the liquid to draw in the liquid region.

RepairTurret.coolEffect: Effect displayed when coolant is used.
RepairTurret.coolantMultiplier: How much healing is increased by with heat capacity.

Liquid.gas: If true, this fluid is treated as a gas (and does not create puddles)
Liquid.color: Color used in pipes and on the ground.
Liquid.gasColor: Color of this liquid in gas form.
Liquid.lightColor: Color used to draw lights. Note that the alpha channel is used to dictate brightness.
Liquid.flammability: 0-1, 0 is completely not flammable, anything above that may catch fire when exposed to heat, 0.5+ is very flammable.
Liquid.temperature: temperature: 0.5 is 'room' temperature, 0 is very cold, 1 is molten hot
Liquid.heatCapacity: how much heat this liquid can store. 0.4=water (decent), anything lower is probably less dense and bad at cooling.
Liquid.viscosity: how thick this liquid is. 0.5=water (relatively viscous), 1 would be something like tar (very slow).
Liquid.explosiveness: how prone to exploding this liquid is, when heated. 0 = nothing, 1 = nuke
Liquid.blockReactive: whether this fluid reacts in blocks at all (e.g. slag with water)
Liquid.coolant: if false, this liquid cannot be a coolant
Liquid.moveThroughBlocks: if true, this liquid can move through blocks as a puddle.
Liquid.incinerable: if true, this liquid can be incinerated in the incinerator block.
Liquid.particleSpacing: Particle effect rate spacing in ticks.
Liquid.boilPoint: Temperature at which this liquid vaporizes. This isn't just boiling.
Liquid.capPuddles: If true, puddle size is capped.
Liquid.hidden: If true, this liquid is hidden in most UI.
Liquid.canStayOn: Liquids this puddle can stay on, e.g. oil on water.

Weather.duration: Default duration of this weather event in ticks.

Weapon.name: displayed weapon region
Weapon.display: whether weapon should appear in the stats of a unit with this weapon
Weapon.useAmmo: whether to consume ammo when ammo is enabled in rules
Weapon.mirror: whether to create a flipped copy of this weapon upon initialization. default: true
Weapon.flipSprite: whether to flip the weapon's sprite when rendering. internal use only - do not set!
Weapon.alternate: whether to shoot the weapons in different arms one after another, rather than all at once; only valid when mirror = true
Weapon.rotate: whether to rotate toward the target independently of unit
Weapon.showStatSprite: Whether to show the sprite of the weapon in the database.
Weapon.baseRotation: rotation at which this weapon starts at.
Weapon.top: whether to draw the outline on top.
Weapon.continuous: whether to hold the bullet in place while firing; it will still require reload.
Weapon.alwaysContinuous: whether this weapon uses continuous fire without reloading; implies continuous = true
Weapon.aimChangeSpeed: Speed at which the turret can change its bullet "aim" distance. This is only used for point laser bullets.
Weapon.controllable: whether this weapon can be aimed manually by players
Weapon.aiControllable: whether this weapon can be automatically aimed by the unit
Weapon.alwaysShooting: whether this weapon is always shooting, regardless of targets ore cone
Weapon.autoTarget: whether to automatically target relevant units in update(); only works when controllable = false.
Weapon.predictTarget: whether to perform target trajectory prediction
Weapon.useAttackRange: if true, this weapon is used for attack range calculations
Weapon.rotateSpeed: rotation speed of weapon when rotation is enabled, in degrees/t
Weapon.inaccuracy: inaccuracy of degrees of each shot
Weapon.shake: intensity and duration of each shot's screen shake
Weapon.recoils: Number of additional counters for recoil.
Weapon.recoilTime: time taken for weapon to return to starting position in ticks. uses reload time by default
Weapon.recoilPow: power curve applied to visual recoil
Weapon.cooldownTime: ticks to cool down the heat region
Weapon.shootX: projectile/effect offsets from center of weapon
Weapon.shootY: projectile/effect offsets from center of weapon
Weapon.x: offsets of weapon position on unit
Weapon.y: offsets of weapon position on unit
Weapon.xRand: Random spread on the X/Y axis.
Weapon.yRand: Random spread on the X/Y axis.
Weapon.shadow: radius of shadow drawn under the weapon; <0 to disable
Weapon.velocityRnd: fraction of velocity that is random
Weapon.extraVelocity: extra velocity that is added as a fraction
Weapon.shootCone: The half-radius of the cone in which shooting will start.
Weapon.rotationLimit: Cone in which the weapon can rotate relative to its mount.
Weapon.minWarmup: minimum weapon warmup before firing (this is not linear, do NOT use 1!)
Weapon.shootWarmupSpeed: lerp speed for shoot warmup, only used for parts
Weapon.smoothReloadSpeed: lerp speed for shoot warmup, only used for parts
Weapon.linearWarmup: If true, shoot warmup is linear instead of a curve.
Weapon.ignoreRotation: whether shooter rotation is ignored when shooting.
Weapon.noAttack: If true, this weapon cannot be used to attack targets.
Weapon.minShootVelocity: min velocity required for this weapon to shoot
Weapon.parentizeEffects: should the shoot effects follow the unit (effects need followParent set to true for this to work)
Weapon.otherSide: internal value used for alternation - do not change!
Weapon.layerOffset: draw Z offset relative to the default value
Weapon.initialShootSound: sound used when this weapon first fires; for continuous weapons only
Weapon.chargeSound: sound used for weapons that have a delay
Weapon.heatRegion: heat region, must be same size as region (optional)
Weapon.cellRegion: cell region, must be same size as region (optional)
Weapon.outlineRegion: outline region to display if top is false
Weapon.mountType: type of weapon mount to be used
Weapon.shootOnDeath: whether this weapon should fire when its owner dies
Weapon.shootOnDeathEffect: If not null and shootOnDeath == true, overrides the weapon's shoot effect only when its owner dies.

DrawRegion.name: If set, overrides the region name.

RepairFieldAbility.sameTypeHealMult: Multiplies healing to units of the same type by this amount.

WallCrafter.drillTime: Time to produce one item at 100% efficiency.
WallCrafter.liquidBoostIntensity: How many times faster the drill will progress when boosted by liquid.
WallCrafter.attribute: Attribute to check for wall output.
WallCrafter.itemBoostIntensity: How many times faster the drill will progress when boosted by items. Note: Using item and liquid boosters at once is not supported.

PowerGenerator.powerProduction: The amount of power produced per tick in case of an efficiency of 1.0, which represents 100%.

RegenAbility.percentAmount: Amount healed as percent per tick.
RegenAbility.amount: Amount healed as a flat amount per tick.

DrawPower.mixcol: If false, fades between emptyRegion and fullRegion instead of mixcol between empty and full colors.

Pump.consumeTime: Interval in-between item consumptions, if applicable.

VariableReactor.unstableSpeed: How quickly instability moves towards 1, per frame.

ParticleEffect.casingFlip: Gives the effect flipping compatability like casing effects.
ParticleEffect.sizeInterp: Particle size interpolation. Null to use interp.
ParticleEffect.sizeFrom: Controls the initial and final sprite sizes.
ParticleEffect.sizeTo: Controls the initial and final sprite sizes.
ParticleEffect.sizeChangeStart: Controls the amount of ticks the effect waits before changing size.
ParticleEffect.useRotation: Whether the rotation adds with the parent

UnitType.envRequired: Environmental flags that are all required for this unit to function. 0 = any environment
UnitType.envEnabled: The environment flags that this unit can function in. If the env matches any of these, it will be enabled.
UnitType.envDisabled: The environment flags that this unit cannot function in. If the env matches any of these, it will explode or be disabled.
UnitType.floorMultiplier: how affected this unit is by terrain
UnitType.baseRotateSpeed: mech base rotation speed in degrees/t
UnitType.hitSize: size of one side of the hitbox square
UnitType.stepShake: shake on each step for leg/mech units
UnitType.rippleScale: ripple / dust size for legged units
UnitType.fallSpeed: how fast this unit falls upon death
UnitType.missileAccelTime: how many ticks it takes this missile to accelerate to full speed
UnitType.armor: incoming damage is reduced by this amount
UnitType.range: minimum range of any weapon; used for approaching targets. can be overridden by setting a value > 0.
UnitType.mineRange: range at which this unit can mine ores
UnitType.buildRange: range at which this unit can build
UnitType.crashDamageMultiplier: multiplier for damage this (flying) unit deals when crashing on enemy things
UnitType.wreckHealthMultiplier: multiplier for health that this flying unit has for its wreck, based on its max health.
UnitType.dpsEstimate: a VERY ROUGH estimate of unit DPS; initialized in init()
UnitType.clipSize: graphics clipping size; <0 to calculate automatically
UnitType.drownTimeMultiplier: multiplier for how slowly this unit drowns - higher numbers, slower drowning.
UnitType.strafePenalty: fractional movement speed penalty for this unit when it is moving in the opposite direction that it is facing
UnitType.researchCostMultiplier: multiplier for cost of research in tech tree
UnitType.groundLayer: for ground units, the layer upon which this unit is drawn
UnitType.flyingLayer: For units that fly, the layer upon which this unit is drawn. If no value is set, defaults to Layer.flyingUnitLow or Layer.flyingUnit depending on lowAltitude
UnitType.payloadCapacity: Payload capacity of this unit in world units^2
UnitType.aimDst: Minimum distance from this unit that weapons can target. Prevents units from firing "inside" the unit.
UnitType.buildBeamOffset: Visual offset of the build beam from the front.
UnitType.mineBeamOffset: Visual offset of the mining beam from the front. Defaults to half the hitsize.
UnitType.targetPriority: WIP: Units of low priority will always be ignored in favor of those with higher priority, regardless of distance.
UnitType.shadowElevation: Elevation of shadow drawn under this (ground) unit. Visual only.
UnitType.shadowElevationScl: Scale for length of shadow drawn under this unit. Does nothing if this unit has no shadow.
UnitType.engineOffset: backwards engine offset from center of unit
UnitType.engineLayer: layer of all engines (<0 for default)
UnitType.itemOffsetY: visual backwards offset of items on unit
UnitType.lightRadius: radius of light emitted, <0 for default
UnitType.softShadowScl: scale of soft shadow - its size is calculated based off of region size
UnitType.fogRadius: fog view radius in tiles. <0 for automatic radius.
UnitType.waveTrailX: horizontal offset of wave trail in naval units
UnitType.waveTrailY: vertical offset of wave trail in naval units
UnitType.trailScl: width of all trails (including naval ones)
UnitType.isEnemy: if true, this unit counts as an enemy in the wave counter (usually false for support-only units)
UnitType.flying: if true, the unit is always at elevation 1
UnitType.wobble: whether this flying unit should wobble around
UnitType.targetAir: whether this unit tries to attack air units
UnitType.targetGround: whether this unit tries to attack ground units
UnitType.faceTarget: if true, this unit will attempt to face its target when shooting/aiming at it
UnitType.circleTarget: AI flag: if true, this flying unit circles around its target like a bomber
UnitType.autoDropBombs: AI flag: if true, this unit will drop bombs under itself even when it is not next to its 'real' target. used for carpet bombers
UnitType.targetBuildingsMobile: For the mobile version only: If false, this unit will not auto-target buildings to attach when a player controls it.
UnitType.canBoost: if true, this unit can boost into the air if a player/processors controls it
UnitType.boostWhenBuilding: if true, this unit will always boost when using builder AI
UnitType.boostWhenMining: if true, this unit will always boost when using miner AI
UnitType.logicControllable: if false, logic processors cannot control this unit
UnitType.playerControllable: if false, players cannot control this unit
UnitType.controlSelectGlobal: If true, the unit can be selected with the global selection hotkey (shift+g).
UnitType.allowedInPayloads: if false, this unit cannot be moved into payloads
UnitType.hittable: if false, this unit cannot be hit by bullets or explosions
UnitType.killable: if false, this unit does not take damage and cannot be kill() / destroy()-ed.
UnitType.targetable: if false, this unit is not targeted by anything.
UnitType.vulnerableWithPayloads: if true, this unit can be hit/targeted when it has payloads (assuming hittable/targetable is false)
UnitType.pickupUnits: if true, this payload unit can pick up units
UnitType.physics: if false, this unit does not physically collide with others.
UnitType.canDrown: if true, this ground unit will drown in deep liquids.
UnitType.useUnitCap: if false, this unit ignores the unit cap and can be spawned infinitely
UnitType.coreUnitDock: if true, this core unit will "dock" to other units, making it re-appear when "undocking".
UnitType.createWreck: if false, no falling "corpse" is created when this unit dies.
UnitType.createScorch: if false, no scorch marks are created when this unit dies
UnitType.lowAltitude: if true, this unit will be drawn under effects/bullets; this is a visual change only.
UnitType.rotateToBuilding: if true, this unit will look at whatever it is building
UnitType.allowLegStep: if true and this is a legged unit, this unit can walk over blocks.
UnitType.legPhysicsLayer: for legged units, setting this to false forces it to be on the ground physics layer.
UnitType.hovering: if true, this unit will not be affected by the floor under it.
UnitType.omniMovement: if true, this unit can move in any direction regardless of rotation. if false, this unit can only move in the direction it is facing.
UnitType.rotateMoveFirst: if true, the unit faces its moving direction before actually moving.
UnitType.healFlash: if true, this unit flashes when being healed
UnitType.canHeal: whether the unit can heal blocks. Initialized in init()
UnitType.singleTarget: if true, all weapons will attack the same target.
UnitType.forceMultiTarget: if true, this unit will be able to have multiple targets, even if it only has one mirrored weapon.
UnitType.canAttack: if false, this unit has no weapons that can attack.
UnitType.hidden: if true, this unit won't show up in the database or various other UIs.
UnitType.internal: if true, this unit is for internal use only and does not have a sprite generated.
UnitType.internalGenerateSprites: For certain units, generating sprites is still necessary, despite being internal.
UnitType.bounded: If false, this unit is not pushed away from map edges.
UnitType.naval: if true, this unit is detected as naval - do NOT assign this manually! Initialized in init()
UnitType.autoFindTarget: if false, RTS AI controlled units do not automatically attack things while moving. This is automatically assigned.
UnitType.targetUnderBlocks: If false, 'under' blocks like conveyors are not targeted.
UnitType.alwaysShootWhenMoving: if true, this unit will always shoot while moving regardless of slowdown
UnitType.hoverable: whether this unit has a hover tooltip
UnitType.alwaysCreateOutline: if true, this modded unit always has a -outline region generated for its base. Normally, outlines are ignored if there are no top = false weapons.
UnitType.generateFullIcon: for vanilla content only - if false, skips the full icon generation step.
UnitType.squareShape: if true, this unit has a square shadow.
UnitType.drawBuildBeam: if true, this unit will draw its building beam towards blocks.
UnitType.drawMineBeam: if true, this unit will draw its mining beam towards blocks
UnitType.drawCell: if false, the team indicator/cell is not drawn.
UnitType.drawItems: if false, carried items are not drawn.
UnitType.drawShields: if false, the unit shield (usually seen in waves) is not drawn.
UnitType.drawBody: if false, the unit body is not drawn.
UnitType.drawSoftShadow: if false, the soft shadow is not drawn.
UnitType.drawMinimap: if false, the unit is not drawn on the minimap.
UnitType.aiController: The default AI controller to assign on creation.
UnitType.controller: Function that chooses AI controller based on unit entity.
UnitType.constructor: Creates a new instance of this unit class.
UnitType.abilities: list of "abilities", which are various behaviors that update each frame
UnitType.weapons: All weapons that this unit will shoot with.
UnitType.immunities: None of the status effects in this set can be applied to this unit.
UnitType.healColor: color that this unit flashes when getting healed (if healFlash is true)
UnitType.lightColor: Color of light that this unit produces when lighting is enabled in the map.
UnitType.deathSound: sound played when this unit explodes (not when it is shot down)
UnitType.wreckSound: sound played when the unit wreck is shot down
UnitType.loopSound: sound played on loop when this unit is around.
UnitType.stepSound: sound played when this mech/insect unit does a step
UnitType.moveSound: sound looped when the unit moves; volume depends on velocity
UnitType.moveSoundPitchMin: pitch of movement sound based on velocity
UnitType.moveSoundPitchMax: pitch of movement sound based on velocity
UnitType.fallEffect: effect that this unit emits when falling
UnitType.fallEngineEffect: effect created at engine when unit falls.
UnitType.deathExplosionEffect: effect created when this unit dies
UnitType.treadEffect: optional effect created when this tank moves
UnitType.useEngineElevation: if false, the thruster is always displayed at its normal size regardless of elevation
UnitType.engineColorInner: color for inner portions of engines
UnitType.trailLength: length of engine trail (if flying) or wave trail (if naval)
UnitType.flowfieldPathType: Cost type ID for flow field/enemy AI pathfinding.
UnitType.pathCost: Function used for calculating cost of moving with ControlPathfinder. Does not affect "normal" flow field pathfinding.
UnitType.pathCostId: ID for path cost, to be used in the control path finder. This is the value that actually matters; do not assign manually. Set in init().
UnitType.sample: A sample of the unit that this type creates. Do not modify!
UnitType.targetFlags: Flags to target based on priority. Null indicates that the closest target should be found. The closest enemy core is used as a fallback.
UnitType.allowChangeCommands: A value of false is used to hide command changing UI in unit factories.
UnitType.commands: Commands available to this unit through RTS controls. An empty array means commands will be assigned based on unit capabilities in init().
UnitType.defaultCommand: Command to assign to this unit upon creation. Null indicates the first command in the array.
UnitType.stances: Stances this unit can have.  An empty array means stances will be assigned based on unit capabilities in init().
UnitType.outlineColor: color for outline generated around sprites
UnitType.outlines: if false, no sprite outlines are generated
UnitType.itemCapacity: amount of items this unit can carry; <0 to determine based on hitSize.
UnitType.ammoCapacity: amount of ammo this unit can hold (if the rule is enabled); <0 to determine based on weapon fire rate.
UnitType.ammoType: ammo this unit uses, if that system is enabled.
UnitType.mineTier: max hardness of ore that this unit can mine (<0 to disable)
UnitType.mineSpeed: mining speed in weird arbitrary units
UnitType.mineWalls: whether this unit can mine ores from floors/walls, respectively
UnitType.mineFloor: whether this unit can mine ores from floors/walls, respectively
UnitType.mineHardnessScaling: if true, harder materials will take longer to mine
UnitType.mineItems: Target items to mine. Used in MinerAI
UnitType.legCount: number of legs this unit has (must have the correct type to function!)
UnitType.legGroupSize: size of groups in which legs move. for example, insects (6 legs) usually move legs in groups of 3.
UnitType.legLength: total length of a leg (both segments)
UnitType.legSpeed: how fast individual legs move towards their destination (non-linear)
UnitType.legForwardScl: scale for how far in front (relative to unit velocity) legs try to place themselves; if legs lag behind a unit, increase this number
UnitType.legBaseOffset: leg offset from the center of the unit
UnitType.legMoveSpace: scaling for space between leg movements
UnitType.legExtension: for legs without "joints", this is how much the second leg sprite is moved "back" by, so it covers the joint region (it's hard to explain without an image)
UnitType.legPairOffset: Higher values of this field make groups of legs move less in-sync with each other.
UnitType.legLengthScl: scaling for how far away legs try to be from the body (not their actual length); e.g. if set to 0.5, legs will appear somewhat folded
UnitType.legStraightLength: if legStraightness > 0, this is the scale for how far away legs are from the body horizontally
UnitType.legMaxLength: maximum length of an individual leg as fraction of real length
UnitType.legMinLength: minimum length of an individual leg as fraction of real length
UnitType.legSplashDamage: splash damage dealt when a leg touches the ground
UnitType.baseLegStraightness: how straight the leg base/origin is (0 = circular, 1 = line)
UnitType.legStraightness: how straight the leg outward angles are (0 = circular, 1 = horizontal line)
UnitType.legBaseUnder: If true, the base (further away) leg region is drawn under instead of over.
UnitType.lockLegBase: If true, legs are locked to the base of the unit instead of being on an implicit rotating "mount".
UnitType.legContinuousMove: If true, legs always try to move around even when the unit is not moving (leads to more natural behavior)
UnitType.flipBackLegs: TODO neither of these appear to do much
UnitType.flipLegSide: TODO neither of these appear to do much
UnitType.emitWalkSound: Whether to emit a splashing noise in water.
UnitType.emitWalkEffect: Whether to emit a splashing effect in water (fasle implies emitWalkSound false).
UnitType.mechLandShake: screen shake amount for when this mech lands after boosting
UnitType.mechStepParticles: whether particles are created when this mech takes a step
UnitType.mechLegColor: color that legs change to when moving, to simulate depth
UnitType.treadRects: list of treads as rectangles in IMAGE COORDINATES, relative to the center. these are mirrored.
UnitType.treadFrames: number of frames of movement in a tread
UnitType.treadPullOffset: how much of a top part of a tread sprite is "cut off" relative to the pattern; this is corrected for
UnitType.crushFragile: if true, 'fragile' blocks will instantly be crushed in a 1x1 area around the tank
UnitType.segmentUnits: TODO wave support - for multi-unit segmented units, this is the number of independent units that are spawned
UnitType.segmentUnit: unit spawned in segments; if null, the same unit is used
UnitType.segmentEndUnit: unit spawned at the end; if null, the segment unit is used
UnitType.segmentLayerOrder: true - parent segments are on higher layers; false - parent segments are on lower layers than head
UnitType.segmentMag: magnitude of sine offset between segments
UnitType.segmentScl: scale of sine offset between segments
UnitType.segmentPhase: index multiplier of sine offset between segments
UnitType.segmentRotSpeed: how fast each segment moves towards the next one
UnitType.segmentSpacing: spacing between separate unit segments (only used for multi-unit worms)
UnitType.segmentRotationRange: rotation between segments is clamped to this range
UnitType.crawlSlowdown: speed multiplier this unit will have when crawlSlowdownFrac is met.
UnitType.crushDamage: damage dealt to blocks under this tank/crawler every frame.
UnitType.crawlSlowdownFrac: the fraction of solids under this block necessary for it to reach crawlSlowdown.
UnitType.homingDelay: ticks that must pass before this missile starts homing.

NuclearReactor.heatOutput: max heat this block can output
NuclearReactor.heatWarmupRate: rate at which heat progress increases
NuclearReactor.ambientCooldownTime: time taken to cool down if no fuel is inputted even if coolant is not present
NuclearReactor.smokeThreshold: threshold at which block starts smoking
NuclearReactor.flashThreshold: heat threshold at which lights start flashing
NuclearReactor.coolantPower: heat removed per unit of coolant

MultiBulletType.repeat: Amount of times the bullet array is repeated.

ForceFieldAbility.cooldown: Cooldown after the shield is broken, in ticks.

MoveLightningAbility.chance: Chance of firing every tick. Set >= 1 to always fire lightning every tick at max speed
MoveLightningAbility.length: Length of the lightning. <= 0 to disable
MoveLightningAbility.minSpeed: Speeds for when to start lightninging and when to stop getting faster
MoveLightningAbility.maxSpeed: Speeds for when to start lightninging and when to stop getting faster
MoveLightningAbility.y: Shifts where the lightning spawns along the Y axis
MoveLightningAbility.heatRegion: Jittering heat sprite like the shield on v5 Javelin
MoveLightningAbility.bullet: Bullet type that is fired. Can be null

EnergyFieldAbility.sameTypeHealMult: Multiplies healing to units of the same type by this amount.

HeatCrafter.overheatScale: After heat meets this requirement, excess heat will be scaled by this number.

Floor.edge: edge fallback, used mainly for ores
Floor.speedMultiplier: Multiplies unit velocity by this when walked on.
Floor.dragMultiplier: Multiplies unit drag by this when walked on.
Floor.damageTaken: Damage taken per tick on this tile.
Floor.drownTime: How many ticks it takes to drown on this. 0 to disable.
Floor.walkEffect: Effect when walking on this floor.
Floor.walkSoundVolume: Volume of sound made when walking.
Floor.walkSoundPitchMin: Volume of sound made when walking.
Floor.walkSoundPitchMax: Volume of sound made when walking.
Floor.drownUpdateEffect: Effect displayed when drowning on this floor.
Floor.status: Status effect applied when walking on.
Floor.liquidDrop: liquids that drop from this block, used for pumps.
Floor.liquidMultiplier: Multiplier for pumped liquids, used for deep water.
Floor.overlayAlpha: for liquid floors, this is the opacity of the overlay drawn on top.
Floor.supportsOverlay: whether this floor supports an overlay floor
Floor.shallow: shallow water flag used for generation
Floor.blendGroup: Group of blocks that this block does not draw edges on.
Floor.oreDefault: Whether this ore generates in maps by default.
Floor.wall: Wall variant of this block. May be Blocks.air if not found.
Floor.decoration: Decoration block. Usually a rock. May be air.
Floor.canShadow: Whether units can draw shadows over this.
Floor.forceDrawLight: If true, this floor ignores the obstructsLight flag of overlays.
Floor.needsSurface: Whether this overlay needs a surface to be on. False for floating blocks, like spawns.
Floor.allowCorePlacement: If true, cores can be placed on this floor.
Floor.wallOre: If true, this ore is allowed on walls.
Floor.blendId: Actual ID used for blend groups. Internal.
Floor.tilingVariants: If >0, this floor is drawn as parts of a large texture.
Floor.autotile: If true, this floor uses autotiling; variants are not supported. See https://github.com/GglLfr/tile-gen
Floor.autotileMidVariants: If >1, the middle region of the autotile has random variants.
Floor.autotileVariants: Variants of the main autotile sprite.
Floor.drawEdgeIn: If true (default), this floor will draw edges of other floors on itself.
Floor.drawEdgeOut: If true (default), this floor will draw its edges onto other floors.

Item.flammability: flammability above 0.3 makes this eligible for item burners.
Item.charge: how electrically potent this item is.
Item.cost: base material cost of this item, used for calculating place times 1 cost = 1 tick added to build time
Item.healthScaling: When this item is present in the build cost, a block's default health is multiplied by 1 + scaling, where 'scaling' is summed together for all item requirement types.
Item.lowPriority: if true, this item is of the lowest priority to drills.
Item.transitionFrames: Number of generated transition frames between each frame
Item.buildable: If true, this material is used by buildings. If false, this material will be incinerated in certain cores.

GenericCrafter.outputItem: Written to outputItems as a single-element array if outputItems is null.
GenericCrafter.outputLiquid: Written to outputLiquids as a single-element array if outputLiquids is null.
GenericCrafter.liquidOutputDirections: Liquid output directions, specified in the same order as outputLiquids. Use -1 to dump in every direction. Rotations are relative to block.
GenericCrafter.dumpExtraLiquid: if true, crafters with multiple liquid outputs will dump excess when there's still space for at least one liquid type
GenericCrafter.legacyReadWarmup: Only used for legacy cultivator blocks.

Effect.startDelay: Time delay before the effect starts
Effect.followParent: If true, parent unit is data are followed.
Effect.rotWithParent: If this and followParent are true, the effect will offset and rotate with the parent's rotation.

UnitCargoUnloadPoint.staleTimeDuration: If a block is full for this amount of time, it will not be flown to anymore.

StatusEffect.damageMultiplier: Damage dealt by the unit with the effect.
StatusEffect.transitionDamage: Damage dealt upon transition to an affinity.
StatusEffect.intervalDamageTime: Spacing (in ticks) between interval damage. <=0 to disable.
StatusEffect.intervalDamagePierce: If true, interval damage is armor piercing.
StatusEffect.parentizeEffect: Should the effect be given a parent.
StatusEffect.permanent: If true, the effect never disappears.
StatusEffect.reactive: If true, this effect will only react with other effects and cannot be applied.
StatusEffect.dynamic: Special flag for the dynamic effect type with custom stats - do not use.
StatusEffect.show: Whether to show this effect in the database.
StatusEffect.effect: Effect that happens randomly on top of the affected unit.
StatusEffect.applyEffect: Effect that is displayed once when applied to a unit.
StatusEffect.applyExtend: Whether the apply effect should display even if effect is already on the unit.
StatusEffect.parentizeApplyEffect: Should the apply effect be given a parent.
StatusEffect.affinities: Affinity & opposite values for stat displays.
StatusEffect.opposites: Affinity & opposite values for stat displays.
StatusEffect.outline: Set to false to disable outline generation.

StaticWall.autotile: If true, this wall uses autotiling; variants are not supported. See https://github.com/GglLfr/tile-gen
StaticWall.autotileMidVariants: If >1, the middle region of the autotile has random variants.

SpawnDeathAbility.spread: Random spread of units away from the spawned.
SpawnDeathAbility.faceOutwards: If true, units spawned face outwards from the middle.

SolidPump.attribute: Attribute that is checked when calculating output.

StackConveyor.recharge: (minimum) amount of loading docks needed to fill a line.

ShieldArcAbility.cooldown: Cooldown after the shield is broken, in ticks.
ShieldArcAbility.whenShooting: If true, only activates when shooting.
ShieldArcAbility.reflectBuildingDamage: Multiplier for reflected bullet building damage. -1 to disable
ShieldArcAbility.reflectVel: Velocity multiplier for reflected bullets on the opposite axis. Negative values = concave, positive values = convex
ShieldArcAbility.missileUnitMultiplier: Multiplier for shield damage taken from missile units.
ShieldArcAbility.drawArc: Whether to draw the arc line.
ShieldArcAbility.region: If not null, will be drawn on top.
ShieldArcAbility.color: Color override of the shield. Uses unit shield colour by default.
ShieldArcAbility.offsetRegion: If true, sprite position will be influenced by x/y.
ShieldArcAbility.pushUnits: If true, enemy units are pushed out.

Turret.targetInterval: Ticks between attempt at finding a target.
Turret.newTargetInterval: Target interval for when this turret already has a valid target. -1 = targetInterval
Turret.consumeAmmoOnce: If true, ammo is only consumed once per shot regardless of bullet count.
Turret.heatRequirement: Minimum input heat required to fire.
Turret.maxHeatEfficiency: Maximum efficiency possible, if this turret uses heat.
Turret.velocityRnd: Fraction of bullet velocity that is random.
Turret.scaleLifetimeOffset: Fraction of lifetime that is added to bullets with lifeScale.
Turret.shootCone: Maximum angle difference in degrees at which turret will still try to shoot.
Turret.xRand: Random spread on the X axis.
Turret.drawMinRange: If true, a range ring is also drawn for minRange.
Turret.trackingRange: Range at which it finds and locks on to the target, but does not shoot.
Turret.minRange: Minimum bullet range. Used for artillery only.
Turret.accurateDelay: If true, this turret will accurately target moving targets with respect to shoot.firstShotDelay.
Turret.moveWhileCharging: If false, this turret can't move while charging.
Turret.reloadWhileCharging: If false, this turret can't reload while charging
Turret.warmupMaintainTime: How long warmup is maintained even if this turret isn't shooting.
Turret.targetAir: If true, this block targets air units.
Turret.targetGround: If true, this block targets ground units and structures.
Turret.targetBlocks: If true, this block targets blocks.
Turret.targetHealing: If true, this block targets friend blocks, to heal them.
Turret.playerControllable: If true, this turret can be controlled by players.
Turret.displayAmmoMultiplier: If true, this block will display ammo multipliers in its stats (irrelevant for certain types of turrets).
Turret.targetUnderBlocks: If false, 'under' blocks like conveyors are not targeted.
Turret.alwaysShooting: If true, the turret will always shoot when it has ammo, regardless of targets in range or any control.
Turret.predictTarget: Whether this turret predicts unit movement.
Turret.unitSort: Function for choosing which unit to target.
Turret.unitFilter: Filter for types of units to attack.
Turret.buildingFilter: Filter for types of buildings to attack.
Turret.heatColor: Color of heat region drawn on top (if found)
Turret.shootEffect: Optional override for all shoot effects.
Turret.smokeEffect: Optional override for all smoke effects.
Turret.ammoUseEffect: Effect created when ammo is used. Not optional.
Turret.shootSound: Sound emitted when a single bullet is shot.
Turret.chargeSound: Sound emitted when shoot.firstShotDelay is >0 and shooting begins.
Turret.loopSound: The sound that this block makes while active. One sound loop. Do not overuse.
Turret.soundPitchMin: Range for pitch of shoot sound.
Turret.soundPitchMax: Range for pitch of shoot sound.
Turret.ammoEjectBack: Backwards Y offset of ammo eject effect.
Turret.linearWarmup: If true, turret warmup is linear instead of a curve.
Turret.recoil: Visual amount by which the turret recoils back per shot.
Turret.recoils: Number of additional counters for recoil.
Turret.recoilTime: ticks taken for turret to return to starting position in ticks. uses reload time by default
Turret.recoilPow: power curve applied to visual recoil
Turret.cooldownTime: ticks to cool down the heat region
Turret.elevation: Visual elevation of turret shadow, -1 to use defaults.
Turret.shake: How much the screen shakes per shot.
Turret.drawer: Defines drawing behavior for this turret.

SectorPreset.isLastSector: If true, this is the last sector in its planetary campaign.
SectorPreset.requireUnlock: If true, this sector must be unlocked before landing is permitted.
SectorPreset.showHidden: If true, the icon and name is shown, even when it's a 'hidden' always-unlocked sector. TODO: this field may be changed, not sure how it should work
SectorPreset.overrideLaunchDefaults: If true, uses this sector's launch fields instead
SectorPreset.allowLaunchSchematics: Whether to allow users to specify a custom launch schematic for this map.
SectorPreset.allowLaunchLoadout: Whether to allow users to specify the resources they take to this map.
SectorPreset.attackAfterWaves: If true, switches to attack mode after waves end.
SectorPreset.originalPosition: The original position of this sector; used for migration. Internal use for vanilla campaign only!
SectorPreset.shieldSectors: Sectors that prevent this sector from being landed on until they are completed.
